/commit.description: Quality-checked commit workflow with tests, lint, type checks, and atomic commits

steps.name: discovery
steps.description: Read AGENTS.md files to identify project-specific commands for testing, linting, migrations, and type checking.

steps.name: quality-safety-checks
steps.description: Run quality and safety checks before committing.

checks.description: Non-trivial changes/fixes should be accompanied by tests. If there are none, prompt the user whether tests should be created and run before continuing.
If models.py changed, ensure migrations are generated and included.
Ensure non-trivial changes maintain type annotation rigor. Run the type checker if it exists.
Scan for hardcoded secrets or leftover debug statements (print, breakpoint).
If new imports are added, verify if pyproject.toml or Nix files need updating.

steps.name: testing
steps: Locate test suite instructions in the README.md or AGENTS.md.
For non-trivial changes or refactors, adjust existing tests or implement new ones.
Run the tests. If they fail, fix the code before committing.

commands.label: Nix flake check (recommended, no local DB needed)
Django test runner
commands.command: cd amc-backend && uv run task test

steps.name: lint
steps.description: Run linters and auto-fix safe issues.

commands.label: Ruff lint via Nix flake check

steps.on_unsafe_fix: Hold the commit and ask the user if a fix is potentially unsafe before applying.

steps.name: commit
steps.description: Review the diff and create atomic commits.
rules: Split unrelated changes (e.g., refactor vs. feat) into separate commits.
